seoTitle: How to Use Timeline View in Trello
metaDescription: Switch your Trello board into Timeline view to map roadmaps, group cards by member or label, and manage unscheduled tasks visually.
intro: Timeline view turns your standard Trello board into a visual roadmap without needing a separate Gantt chart tool. This guide covers switching to Timeline, grouping cards, adjusting date ranges, and handling tasks that don't have dates yet.

steps.heading: Switch to Timeline view
steps.body: Click the view switcher dropdown in the top-left of your board and select Timeline.

steps.heading: Review the calendar grid
steps.body: Your scheduled cards will now appear as horizontal bars across a calendar-style layout.

steps.heading: Group by member or label
steps.body: Open the Group By dropdown and select Member to isolate individual workloads, or choose a label to group by category.

steps.heading: Adjust your zoom level
steps.body: Click the zoom header to toggle between weeks, months, and quarters depending on how much detail you need.

steps.heading: Resize task bars to adjust dates
steps.body: Click and drag the edge of a card bar to stretch it across multiple days, which automatically updates the card's due date.

steps.heading: Schedule unscheduled cards
steps.body: Open the Unscheduled side drawer to find cards without dates, then drag them onto the timeline grid to schedule them.

faq.question: Do I need a paid plan to use Timeline view?
faq.answer: Yes, Timeline view is available on Trello's Premium and Enterprise plans.

faq.question: Can I create new cards directly in Timeline view?
faq.answer: Yes, double-clicking an empty space in a team member's lane instantly generates a new task card.

faq.question: How do I reassign a task to a different person?
faq.answer: Drag the task vertically from one member's lane to another to reassign the workload.

faq.question: How do I jump back to today's date?
faq.answer: Click the Today button in the header to reset your view axis to the current date.
